## Phase 7: Session Lifecycle — Disconnect Detection & Auto-Reconnect

### Context

When a chat WebSocket disconnects (server restart, idle timeout, network drop, ACP subprocess crash), the user sees nothing — input just disables silently with "Waiting for response..." placeholder. Sending a new message silently fails because `ws.readyState !== OPEN` in `sendUserMessage()`. The user has no idea what happened.

The fix is **frontend-only** (`chat.js`). The backend already supports session resume via `sessionId` in the `start` message — no server changes needed.

### Problem: Three gaps in chat.js

1. **`ws.onclose` (line 194)** — sets `streaming=false` and updates input, but shows NO visual indicator to user
2. **`sendUserMessage()` (line 527)** — silently drops the message when WS is closed; the `if (ws.readyState === OPEN)` guard has no else branch
3. **No reconnection** — once disconnected, the tab is permanently dead

### Changes to `chat.js`

**7.1 — Track disconnected state in tabData**

Add `disconnected: false` to the tabData object in `createChatTab()` (line 132).

**7.2 — Show "Session disconnected" on close**

Replace `ws.onclose` handler (lines 194-197):
```javascript
ws.onclose = function () {
  tabData.streaming = false;
  tabData.disconnected = true;
  finalizeAssistantMessage(tabData);
  appendSystemMessage(tabData, "Session disconnected");
  updateInputState();
};
```

**7.3 — Auto-reconnect when user sends a message**

In `sendUserMessage()`, replace the `if (ws.readyState === OPEN)` block (lines 527-531) with reconnect logic:

```javascript
if (tab.ws && tab.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
  // Normal path: WS alive, send directly
  tab.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "user_message", text: text }));
  tab.streaming = true;
  updateInputState();
} else if (tab.disconnected && tab.sessionId) {
  // Reconnect path: WS dead, have session ID — reconnect and queue message
  tab.pendingMessage = text;
  tab.disconnected = false;
  appendSystemMessage(tab, "Reconnecting...");
  connectWebSocket(tab, tab.sessionId);
} else {
  // No session ID to resume — can't reconnect
  appendSystemMessage(tab, "Cannot reconnect — no session to resume");
}
```

**7.4 — Send queued message after reconnection**

In `handleServerMessage()`, after the `session_init` case (line 215), flush any pending message:

```javascript
case "session_init":
  tabData.sessionId = msg.sessionId;
  if (msg.agentInfo) {
    appendSystemMessage(tabData, msg.agentInfo.title + " connected");
  }
  if (!msg.historyLoaded && tabData.sessionSummary) {
    appendInfoBanner(tabData, "Previous messages not available. " + tabData.sessionSummary);
  }
  // Flush queued message from reconnect
  if (tabData.pendingMessage) {
    var pending = tabData.pendingMessage;
    tabData.pendingMessage = null;
    tabData.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "user_message", text: pending }));
    tabData.streaming = true;
    updateInputState();
  }
  break;
```

**7.5 — Update `updateInputState()` to reflect disconnected state**

```javascript
function updateInputState() {
  var tab = activeChatTabId ? chatTabs[activeChatTabId] : null;
  var streaming = tab && tab.streaming;
  var disconnected = tab && tab.disconnected;
  $chatInput.disabled = streaming;
  $chatSend.disabled = streaming;
  $chatInput.placeholder = streaming
    ? "Waiting for response..."
    : disconnected
      ? "Send a message to reconnect..."
      : "Send a message...";
}
```

### Summary of state transitions

```
[connected] --ws.onclose--> [disconnected]
  - Show "Session disconnected" message
  - Input placeholder: "Send a message to reconnect..."

[disconnected] --user sends message--> [reconnecting]
  - Show "Reconnecting..." message
  - Queue the message in tabData.pendingMessage
  - Open new WebSocket with sessionId

[reconnecting] --session_init received--> [connected]
  - Show "Agent connected" message
  - Flush pendingMessage automatically
  - Normal streaming begins

[reconnecting] --error/close--> [disconnected]
  - Show error message
  - User can try again
```

### Files modified
- `src/agent_kitchen/static/chat.js` — all changes (sections 7.1–7.5)

1. Open a chat session, send a message, wait for response
2. Kill the server (Ctrl+C) — should see "Session disconnected" in chat
3. Restart server, type a new message, hit Enter — should see "Reconnecting..." then the response streams
4. Test with idle timeout: leave session open, come back after ACP process dies, send message — should auto-reconnect
